reviews.review_text:
We stayed here for a week and would have stayed longer if we had the time.
The staff, facilities and area are all great.
I would highly recommend this park.
We stayed in our campervan, but the rooms/cabins looked awesome
Stayed there over the May long weekend and could not fault the park

Tallebudgera Creek Tourist Park is perfect for families. We stay here regularly because of the amazing staff, great amenities, cleanliness, and the endless things for the kids to do.

The creek is perfect for throwing the paddle boards in and paddling away, while the pool and slides keep everyone entertained. I even joined the kids on the slides — so much fun!

It’s also super easy to get vans and campers in and out, and the whole place feels safe and welcoming.

We absolutely love Tallebudgera Creek Tourist Park and can’t wait to come back. See you next time!
Tallebudgeerah Tourist Park Gold Coast Queensland is a fantastic place regardless of the weather—be it rain or  shine 🌧️☀️🤓

We love it here. We were extremely pleased with the excellent and friendly service you can only get from a family run business. We enjoyed: the views from our waterfront cabins and the greenery in the park, cooking on our BBQ with seaviews, looking for crabs during low tide, the heated pool with fun slides after a refreshing dip in the creek, convenient parking, the food kiosk & truck and the places you can explore on the pedestrian path along the beach in all directions - to the left, right, across the bridge. Bring conventional scooters/bikes for the kids!

The cabins itself are clean but could use with another round of mopping.
Hermoso lugar para pasar unos días relajado.

This year was our seventh year coming here and we absolutely love the park. However  very disappointed with the effort made enforcing the park rules.
There is a total ban on electric bikes and scooters, no riding after dark and all kids must wear helmets.
They’re literally electric bikes and scooters  everywhere. No kids are wearing helmets and there are bikes and scooters everywhere after dark.
The whole stay we were genuinely concerned for the safety of our children and my elderly parents.
The security guard driving round at night is doing nothing to enforce these rules and the text message sent to parent seems to be completely ineffective.
I’m very concerned that it’s going to take somebody seriously getting hurt before anything is done about this.
A lovely park right by the creek. We enjoyed the lovely pools, and delicious coffee from the cafe. The ensuite bathroom was perfect, highly recommending this park.
Great park, well maintained and a great location. great for a swim every day and just a short trip to Burleigh heads for resturants, cafes, food and supplies
What a amazing place to stay 13 years |I have made some amazing friends that we know call family.
Great location and activities. Friendly, helpful staff.
I'm in caravan.  But I do believe the accommodation is very good. Close to shops. Public transport right outside the park. 10 minute walk to the ocean

Incredible park and location but wouldn’t recommend the unpowered camp sites.
They were tiny and we had a tree in the middle of ours so it was challenging to fit.
The noise from the road at night was absolutely horrendous and the bathrooms were miles away.
The cabins and powered camp sites are epic though. Worth paying for the better sites.

Location location location!! This park is amazing. Best location right on the creek, grounds are kept looking tidy, amenities were clean, kids loved the pool and slides and the reception staff at check in were so welcoming and friendly. They even have a fully stocked kiosk! Thanks for making our family stay so much fun.
Such a great park, my 6yo is up and out the door as soon as he opens his eyes... bikes, creek and pool with some park activities on school holidays. Not much better then Tally creek when it's on.
We had the best Easter at Tallebudgera caravan park over Easter - family friendly , great layout and amenities , 2 pools - kids loved the water slides and the friendliest staff - highly recommend
Clean Park, all of the Staff are friendly

Great Location on the iconic Tallebudgera Creek. Close to shops, ocean Beach, scenic walks and Surf Club with fantastic views and food. We had an ensuite Powered Caravan Site and it was large and well maintained.|Staff were very friendly and more than helpful
Stellar spot, staff and on site kiosk great. The kids and adults loved it! Even at Easter the park was clean and even had security.
Iconic location close to Beach, scenic walks and surf club with good food and cold drinks.
Beautiful clean happy venue with space to enjoy the coast
Amazing site, the amenities were always clean and well looked after. No issues at all. Pool is amazing. 5*

This is hands down one of the best caravan parks around. The staff are friendly, accommodating and always ready to help. Scott is also a legend and managed to help us out and made sure we had the best family stay! Thanks team!
Scott’s awesome
We stayed in a Cabin here for 2 nights. Great location, helpful and friemdly staff. Csnin overlooked the River.
Tallebudgera Tourist Park lived up to all our previous visits.   Incredible cabins, facilities and staff.   Can’t wait for our next stay
One of the best parks we have stayed in along the coast. Close enough but removed from the glitzy GC insanity. Awesome facilities, super staff.|We will be returning when we next venture up the coast.

We spent 2 nights here with a bunch of friends all camping. We chose an ensuite site for convenience. Awesome spot, right on the water. Kids went from beach, fishing, pool and scootering the whole time.

Beautiful location, lovely staff, but unfortunately the cabins are outdated, dirty, bathrooms are full of mould, damp, sticky, sand on the furniture and beds, etc… wouldn’t stay here again so no I do not recommend… we’ve stayed at multiple tourist parks around Australia and have our favorites, this one wasn’t up to standard.

Location is great. Hubby arranged a cabin for the weekend for a short staycation but the cabin was not very clean,  one single bunk had sand and socks in it, which was concerning, the shower had mould in the corners and the bottom of the glass was cloudy and if you scraped your nail on it, it came off,  2 of the blankets were dirty. We did tell reception who said they would get the sheets changed but we just put our daughter on the couch when they didn’t come.
Enjoyed my stay here and will definitely be back in the future
Nice and clean. Staff were very friendly and helpful. Beautiful spot on the creek. Lots of places to walk.  Loved the adult pool
EXCELLENT FACILITIES AND GREAT STAFF
Really nice sized caravan sites. Plenty of room to back a van in. Ample size to fit the van and car. Well maintained sites. Great amenities. Great pool. Great for kids. Will definitely return.

We loved our time at Tallebudgera Creek Caravan park. Our caravan site was well situated near the beach. We loved walking around the creek with a coffee in hand. We enjoyed the warm pool and the kids were never bored.
Tallebudgera’s Creek is one of our favourite parks. The location is perfect, the amenities first rate and staff communicative, so helpful and lovely!

Nice location, but unfortunately the experience was ruined by the staff. Their attitude was rude and unprofessional, which made the stay uncomfortable from start to finish. Hospitality should make guests feel welcome, not like a burden.

Do yourself a favour and book somewhere else.
Loved it and would definately stay again.
Such a great location for a caravan park!

The amenities were disgusting.  We stayed 3 weeks, I informed them of a toilet that did not flush and a broken toilet seat, and after 3 weeks still not fixed.
The shower heads are so corroded that you need to either dance around or stand against the wall to get wet.  I understand you are on the beach and corrosion happens, but so should maintenance.
Gutters need clearing so you don’t get soaked from the rain at the bathroom entrance while your entering the code to get in.

One plus, you are right on Talle Creek, which is beautiful.  Just don’t look behind you to the park, major disappointment.

Overall a huge disappointment for the Gold Coast.

Es ist ein großer Park mit toller Lage. Die Toiletten und Duschen sind okay (obwohl die Duschen für so manche große Frau zu niedrig sind). Der Pool ist sehr schön und macht viel Spaß für groß und klein. Leider war am Morgen viel Enten💩 am und im Pool. Der Preis ist angemessen (wir bezahlten pro Nacht 77 $ powered/2 Adults). Das einzige was mich wirklich geärgert hat, war die Art und Weise wie man darauf hingewiesen wurde, ja um 10:00 Uhr raus zu sein. 09:45 Uhr kam einer angefahren und wies uns etwas unfreundlich darauf hin. Darum auch ein Stern weniger.

One of the worst campground I ever stayed on. 70 Dollars for an unpowered tentsite is already a lot but okay.
But then you get absolutely nothing from the campsite. It’s right next to the big road so it’s loud throughout the night.
The camp kitchen is an absolute joke. No equipment there.. not even a simple cooking plate.
The tent sites are right next to the playground so at 6am you have screaming children practically next to your ear.
Additionally the lights of the main house and the kiosk next to the playground stay on throughout the night so the tent is bright as daylight throughout the night.
For that amount of money you would think that at least the bathrooms would be nice.. but no. The toilets are either broken or dirty, the shower is too low so you can’t really stand under it if you’re over 185cm and everything is just dirty.
All in all one of the worst nights we had in three months of travelling through Australia.
We stayed here over the busy Australia Day / school holidays period and it was fantastic. The location is awesome, staff are so friendly and the park was really clean and tidy.
fantastic location, camp kitchens compared to other parks is quite poor.

Beautiful spot on the water, a short stroll to swim in creek . Easy access for fishing. Walk easily to ocean and burleigh heads , restaurants and shops . 2 pools ..one adults only and a very good kid pool with lots of slides and entertainment for the kids . A food van on site at weekends and a kiosk that sells beach wear to food to alcohol . highly recommend. very family friendly

Very big but good camping. Enough space and quiet at night. Just beside the palm beach. You can walk 1 km to a shopping street and restaurants in Burleigh. Bought lots of nice things. There is a Woolworth too and a bws for wine. The pools are amazing and there is one for adults which is fantastic. Reception is friendly. I would recommend for location and beach and pools. The amenities are good but in the morning a little dirty with all the sand.

Large park, has good sized sites for larger caravans. Friendly staff.
Clean amenities, camp kitchens and 3 pools
We had a ensuite site and our daughter and family had a cabin both were fantastic could not have wished for better

This has by far been the worst caravan park we have stayed at during travelling Australia.
The service was disgustingly rude.
We found drug paraphernalia in the ammenities.
We listened to the workers cart bins around on the back of their buggies at ridiculous hours of the morning.
Even had a mess around with extra money being taken out of our account and having to wait 3 business days for the refund and the reception was incredibly rude even though the mistake was their end:
Absolutely appalling.
Amazing location on the river, highly recommend staying here

We have stayed a lot of caravan parks over a long period of time and Tallebudgera is definitely one of the best places we find to relax and unwind, the staff are so friendly and always happy to help site is so clean and not to mention one of the best places on the Gold Coast thank you guys thanks for looking after us George and Kerri.

Ark facilities are great but don't book the unpowered tent sites, they are right on the highway and its like sleeping next to a race track, cars screaming up and down all night, very unpleasant. Had to pay for minimum 2 night stay, vould only handle one night and no refund for the paid night
Great spot. Few rules but they aren't over the top. Kids pool with slides and an adults only pool. Park backs onto the creek. Not to far to walk to Burleigh either 👌

We stayed at a 2 bedroom cabin on the water and wow the view and location is just incredible. Not many other holiday parks quite like this one with quality and clean amenities along with ideal access to a kid friendly beach as well as pool and water play area. The customer service from the staff here, particularly Andrew and Scott, was excellent and they helped to provide timely solutions to some issues we were experiencing in our cabin which was greatly appreciated. Kiosk on site for food as well as a small general store which was really handy for the essentials. You can’t go wrong booking here and we hope to be back one day.

Our favourite caravan park for fun days at the  beautiful Talle Creek. Perfect spot right in the middle of the Gold Coast. Clean amenities, activities on the holidays and a very reasonably priced kiosk make this a great casual family spot.
Good amenities, great location and probably the best value compared to some on the gold coast.

I have been staying at Tallebudgera Creek Tourist Park over the Christmas and New Year period for 27 years,  staying for 3 - 4 weeks. It truly feels like a second home. The staff are absolutely amazing — always friendly, welcoming, and incredibly helpful, which makes every stay even more enjoyable.
The amenities are consistently spotless and very well maintained, no matter how busy the park is. The activities organised within the park are fantastic and create such a great community atmosphere, especially during the holiday season. I also love the variety of food trucks that come through at different times — it’s a great touch and something we really look forward to.
The location is simply unbeatable. You can walk to the calm waters of Tallebudgera Creek, head the other way to the surf beach, stroll to the surf club, or wander to nearby cafés — everything is right at your doorstep.
Everything about this park is fantastic. After nearly three decades of returning year after year, I couldn’t recommend Tallebudgera Creek Tourist Park highly enough.
Great place to stay! Amenities are very clean, staff are friendly and accommodating. Location is the best on the gc highly recommend!

Tallebudgera is one of my favourite places to camp...my kids were on rotation from the beach to the creek to the pool, then repeat! Amenities are clean, and the staff super-helpful, even though it was really busy over festive season. We will be back!

We traveled from Sydney to Brisbane and had a stopover here as well, I can confidentally say this was the best campsite on the coast in between S and B. Easy check in, nice staff, facilities are nice and clean, nice pool and kitchen area. It was pretty full but did not feel crowdy,  plenty of options nearby for different beaches the creek, hiking on Burley's. you can buy almost everything in the Kiosk where staff was also nice. We can't wait to stay there next time.

Been here a few nights paying $100 per night for our tent...unpowered.

And they closed the pool at 5:45 p.m. for a storm that never came...

Feeling ripped off, we only came to this site for our kids to enjoy the pool area...

A 30 minute swim would have been all we needed.

Look at the photos, 30 minutes apart, 1st photo before, 2nd after.
Zero consideration for customers coming back from activities in the late afternoon.

THERE WAS NEVER A CONCERN FOR ANYONE'S SAFETY, THAT CHESTNUT WON'T FLY, AND IT WASN'T SUNSET.

GET A WEATHER APP.

Not bad very beautiful nice area awesome views but very expensive
An always packed
Absolutely stunning I loved it

This was our first stay, what a wonderful park. Easy checkin, cruisey vibe, everyone very respectful. Access to beach, creek, pools and all sort of other activities makes this an absolute win win location. Wish we could have stayed for longer

This the best swims for young families. Loved how dare devil teens attempted the bridge jump and crossing currents to make it across to the other side. Get in quick and early to find perfect parking as it is quite busy during the summer months.
Best place for holidays

We stayed at Tallebudgera Creek holiday Park at the end of November 25 on a powered site .We stayed Friday ,Saturday and paid for Sunday nite so we could leave late that night and travel home in the cool.  .The park and amenities were awesome the adult only pool is great to have when you just want to chill without being splashed at . .then you also have the beach front still water it was just a really lovely park clean and staff very friendly. .we wanted to have a BBQ at the pool side under the picnic hut the staff got us a fresh gas bottle and moved the BBQ closer to the hut for us to make it easier. .The girls behind the counter were all very friendly and helpful .  we will be back again  next year.

Our recent stay at Tallebudgera Creek Caravan Park was unfortunately overshadowed by an incident that raises real concerns about the professionalism of the staff.

Checkout is at 10:00 AM, and as first-time campervan users, it took us longer than expected to pack up. We received two calls from reception, and we informed them both times that we were already in the process of leaving and doing our best.

At around 11:15 AM, a male staff member arrived at our site. Instead of offering assistance, patience, or simply a polite reminder, he chose to speak to us in a dismissive and confrontational way. His exact words were:

“We will put a note on your file, so don’t bother booking us again.”

This was entirely unnecessary and deeply unprofessional. A hospitality business should never make guests feel unwelcome, especially when they are clearly trying to comply and when the situation could have been handled with basic courtesy.

We fully acknowledge the checkout time. What we cannot accept is the attitude. There was no attempt to understand, no offer of help, and no respect shown in the interaction. Being told not to return—by a staff member whose job is to represent the park—was astonishing.

Because of this, we will not be returning, and we hope management takes this seriously. A single conversation delivered with respect could have changed the entire experience. Instead, it revealed a service culture that requires urgent attention.

Lin

Here is the response after seeing yours.
First, I was not sitting and having breakfast when staff came to the site. My three young kids had eaten earlier; I was already on the ground packing up the campervan as fast as I could. I fully accept that we were late checking out – that was our mistake.

Second, I strongly disagree with the claim that I was “aggressive and disrespectful.” The staff member approached with a very impatient attitude, and my response was simply, “Yes, you’ve reminded us for the third time, I know,” while I kept packing. I did not swear, yell or threaten anyone. Our neighbour directly opposite us, Steve (who checked out the same Monday), witnessed the whole interaction and can confirm this.

I understand enforcing checkout times, but being unfairly labelled as aggressive and then told I’m not welcome back, based solely on one staff member’s version of events, is extremely upsetting. Because of the way this was handled, I will not be returning.
Amazing spot for a long weekend, highly recommend 😍

We’ve stayed here a few times usually 2 weeks at a time and have booked to stay again. Great location, great walks, right on the water, great facilities, clean amenities and fabulous staff. Always great to stay here .. pool and shop onsite, bbqs and all the usual things you’d expect. Easy check in as well. Definitely our favourite spot and now we nay live 40 min away but still the perfect holiday
We had a great time camping at the park! We will definitely be back for more.  We love this place!!!

Had a wonderful few nights camping at the park, so good that we booked for 2 more longer stays. The staff went out of their way to help with accommodation and where to shop. The park was kept mowed and clean. Thoroughly enjoyed it. Looking forward to our next stay.

Staff made mistake and put us a long way from our group. Beautiful spot. Is extremely busy though.
Cabin was good, but air-conditioning in bedrooms would be good and as we were given a disabled cabin there was dog hair everywhere from what I assume would be a support animal.
Great access to safe water and nice beach. Park amenities are super clean site are well maintained and good size. Big improvements on water and electricity of sites good slab and driveway accessible
Good park.  Expensive in both off peak and peak times.

Great park, amenities were kept nice and clean, kids loved the pools and trikes. The kiosk and food truck were also fantastic. Staff were kind and always friendly. Close proximity to beach, cafes and surf living saving club.

Staff was so rude and very aggressive then try to say that my caravan was 9.9m (32ft) I have a single wheeler Jayco pop top that I bought new and it’s around 16ft plus drawbar wouldn’t recommend to anyone would rather go sleep on the side of the road than come here
Caravan site was perfect, along the board walk.  Waterviews were magical.

Fantastic holiday park.
Great service from the staff and facilities were always clean and well maintained.
Pool is heated and opens early which was great for the kids.
Beach is safe and easily accessed with lovely walking paths.
Highly recommended.

Thanks Telly team

Beautiful location on the banks of the Tallebudgera Creek, with lovely walks along the creek to the ocean absolutely stunning.
Easy check in, friendly staff, great amenities. Would highly recommend!

Everything about this park was excellent. The staff, location, sites, amenities and features were all perfect. Was the perfect stay for my family and I and was in the perfect location to get to all the tourist attractions in and around Gold Coast (sea world etc). Will be coming back in the future.

SHOUT OUT TO MANAGER OF PARK- thank you.

I had to cancel at very short notice, our booked van site due to my husband’s sickness and we were prepared to forgo our $$ paid.

The manager rung and offered to hold our $$ in credit which we did not expect.
Such great service by management and team.

We look forward to visiting Tallebudgera soon.
L & B
Bribie Island

Upon arrival, needed to wait for cabin.
39deg. Windy
Sat in office.
Staff wonderful invited us to have coffee etc.
Cabins immaculate. All staff onsite helpful and polite. Kiosk staff lovely 👏👏👌❣️
Staff are very helpful. Absolutely beautiful place to stay.

We had the best time! The kids loved the kiosk, rental trikes, heated pool and slides, and the adults loved the proximity to the beach and nature. There were sightings of rainbow lorikeets, iguanas, bin chickens, and bush turkeys every day. We stayed in a cabin, and it was handy to have a washer and dryer for a longer stay. Wish there was salt and pepper in addition to the tea bags and instant coffee in the kitchen, but that’s a small thing. The staff were very helpful and gave us a free membership to the nearby surf club. Would return in a heartbeat!

I can't recommend this park enough...... great for families. A short walk to the beach, and even close to kids safe swimming areas. The park has great and clean amenities, and the pools were a fantastic option after the beaches. I'll definitely be coming back.
Great park beautiful location great pool for kids
This is a great outdoors area for gatherings,  birthday parties etc. Need to get there very early as it's very popular.

Be very careful booking a site here you pay for a premium site and are placed on any site available staff are not helpful and don't care and loyalty means nothing do not go to this park unless you are prepared to be treated badly
Nice place to stay.  Will be back.

I had a great experience at the kiosk today! The young guy working there made me a really good coffee—seriously one of the best I’ve had in a while. On top of that, his customer service was excellent. Super friendly, welcoming, and professional. It’s always nice to start the day with not just good coffee, but good vibes too. Keep up the great work!
First time here mainly to catch up with friends.Grey nomads amongst all the families.Wonderful spot for all

Location is ideal, beautiful walks at sunset, food trucks with affordable prices, and friendly people. Pool is perfect for kids and adults. Third time staying here and will come again. Will bring bicycles next time, super family friendly with kids riding bikes all over during school holidays. Book early, sites run out for holidays. Can't give it five stars because they do have maintenance to perform in bathrooms and only good for families, wouldn't come as an adult without kids. Also, tried to book cabins in late July and over the phone they said the bookings didn't open until 1 Sept, and they meant for next year.... I tried again mid August just in case and they said the same thing, so misunderstood twice that I wanted cabins for this year - so I missed out.
School holidays, to many people with children and very expensive.

A perfect place to stay with kids and families, right in the river and a short walk to the beach. Pool was amazing. You have the best of all worlds when it comes to water fun.
Clean and close to everything you could need.

Would love to return but would love to try the cabins next time.

Been coming here for 55 yrs and the whiting are still running up the back of the creek like they were when I was a kid. Love coming back here - stayed in the cabins this time round and was everything that I thought they would be. Would recommend for all family’s

My wife and I are one of the many Victorian couples who spend the cold winter months on the Gold Coast. We have stayed at Tallebudgerra Creek Tourist Park the past six years and would not contemplate going anywhere else.
The location of the park is perfect with Burleigh Heads a fifteen minute walk away for shopping and hospitality venues. Tallebudgerra Creek runs along the side of the park and is great for paddle boarding, canoeing and swimming. A short walk under the highway gets you to Palm Beach.
The facilities at the park are excellent and well maintained and plenty of big sites for extra room. There is an adult only heated pool along with two other pools for the family fun.
The staff are very friendly and welcoming and make sure all of your needs are met.
We highly recommend the park for either short or long term stays. Garry and Annie
Great place to stay! Perfect for families
Always love visiting this beautiful beach.
Just really good
Great service very friendly staff the best place to stay we loved it 😀 👍
Excellent caravan park

Great service, we stayed in a 2 bedroom waterfront cabin. Beautiful views, very clean, spacious and the beds were very comfortable.  Playground for the kids next to the food truck where we ordered fish n chips for lunch was a bonus especially after a swim in the huge pool. Absolutely loved our stay. Highly recommend!
Awesome park. Paid $5 extra for a large site - well worth it. Only 10 minutes to the heart of Surfers. Beautiful walks from the park. Lots of food options nearby
Camped on the waterfront.  Beautiful place.  Walks to do, beaches to visit, shopping at Burleigh too!  No complaints.

Really nice caravan Park
Would recommend it.
Easy check in, great site and facilities and friendly staff.
Fantastic place nice and clean with facilities close to hand. Large sights for big vans.

Upon arriving at this park for the fourth time over the years we were booked into a corner van site which unfortunately was very difficult to access .Unknown to us Tim the owner was watching and didn’t hesitate coming over and assisting us ; after checking the office he offered us a much bigger site and could not have been more helpful.We love this park great location and it has much to offer .Thank you very much Tim and the ladies in the office for your friendly service and always willing to assist us
Highly recommend this park.

What an awesome park, very close to the Talle Creek & Beach. We had a very relaxing, peaceful, 2 weeks in our caravan. The staff are very friendly. Grounds well maintained. Loved the idea that on arrival you drive straight  to your site, set up & then go back to reception & check in. Brilliant. Amenities block very clean & cleaned often even though they are a little dated. Would definitely stay here again.
Simple but beautiful, staff friendly, stayed 2 weeks and loved waking up to the sunrise :)

Perfect location, great views, close to shops and Taller Creek, the recreation centre and super friendly staff  and amenities are perfect for what we needed. It also feels very secure having a boom gate to enter and exit putting us at ease about vehicle safety.

Loved it here! We stayed for a week and were pleasantly surprised by how nice the rooms were. & There are so many different "types" of rooms, something to suit everyone!

We loved it also as we stayed in the winter time, and each day the cabins and the heater kept it soo toasty! Everything was clean and the people were super nice and easy to communicate with.

Would definitely come back!
great location close by to the beach, hikes and food spots. friendly staff and great amenities ! waterfront cabins have a nice view

The natural beauty of the creek is amazing. Crystal clear water has to be seen to be believed. Highly organised, and well-staffed park. 2 adults and a 5 year-old  $100 per night on drive-thru site during June/July holidays. Cancellation policy is worth reading, with full payment required at phone  booking time; well in advance.
We are staying for a week for school holidays it has been a great park so far.

Location = 5/5 • Waterfront Superior Cabin = 3/5
Being on the Tully River was great, awesome location, have always wanted to stay in one of the cabins so was happy when it worked out for this weekend. We stayed for 3 nights in a 2 Bedroom Waterfront Superior Cabin. We were unfortunately very annoyed and disappointed with the lack of privacy in the cabin. There are NO blinds/curtains on the bathroom window at all - meaning at night time you could see into the bathroom clearly despite the window having a glaze on it. See our photo attached) We couldn’t stand in the bathroom naked before or after showering! It’s ridiculous. Also no blockout blinds (only light filtering) on the large loungeroom and kitchen windows so again at night anyone could see in clearly. We had walking paths on both sides of our cabin that campers used often which took them right past our windows at head height. Could even see directly into neighbours cabin windows. Noone wants that. Privacy is the bare minimum you expect in accommodation. And for the price of these cabins, you should get it.
Another issue was no wifi at all. And noone notified us of this at time of booking or checking in. So while the cabin was spacious, and had mostly everything we needed, the privacy was a real issue and the reason we unfortunately would not book and stay again.
Unreal location and park. Well done to the staff and management. Great experience all round!

Location is amazing ! Absolutely stunning and close to so many cool locations.
Service by the staff at the Park is awesome from Reception to the groundsman.
Park facilities were great. Nice and clean areas.
Beware that the public can access the Park from the foreshore so locking belongings up at night is a must.
The cabin was ordinary for the price I paid. Very small rooms and bathroom. Old fittings, old mould under silicone in the bathroom, most window coverings were broken in some way. Bunks are tiny and beds are not very comfy, pillows are thin and uncomfortable. Kitchen facilities are basic but clean and adequate.
Overall, the value for money was for the view and the location, not the cabin.

What an amazing park - from the great location, great service, easy check-in, friendly and happy staff to the great amenities, ease of access to both the creek and the ocean, easy walk to the surf club for dinner. Loved the little kiosk for a quick morning coffee too. The sites are a good size and the park has a friendly and relaxed atmosphere - super nice staff all round. Even though the weather was not the best we really enjoyed our stay and look forward to coming back for a longer stay.

Our 3 week stay at Tallebudgera Creek Tourist Park was wonderful, all staff members were very friendly and helpful. Park facilities are being upgraded with the occasional water outage for short periods, we were given plenty of notice so we could plan our day with little or no disruption.
Great
Scott and the team went above and beyond for our stay. The facilities are well kept and plenty of activities for the kids. Love our stay!

Sehr schön gelegener Campingplatz mit kurzen Wegen zum Strand. Der Platz bietet alles was man braucht und es ist alles sauber. Es gibt auch Schwimmbecken, diese haben wir aber wegen der Nähe zum Strand nicht genutzt.
Buchung und Check-in waren sehr einfach. Wir haben uns sehr wohl gefühlt und kommen gerne wieder.

I live locally and booked a site for my well travelled parents.
I couldn't have been happier with Tallebudgera Creek Tourist Park. Everything from the team friendliness and helpfulness, streamlined communications, the facilities, easy access to everything, the onsite food truck and the list could go on. That is before noting how beautiful the creek and walking track around to the beach are! The location is excellent.
From the moment I called I was greeted warmly and offered different options before settling on a waterfront site, which was spectacular. Then on arrival my parents experienced a friendly and helpful team and easy access to their site. They said the sites at this park were above average in size and that was a nice treat for them. The bathroom facilities were clean and the site was super clean which was testament to the team given it very clearly was fully booked and super busy during school holiday period! Thanks to the whole team for making my parents stay a treat. I'll be next booking for some friends to come visit with their kids! And I'm confident my parents will visit me again :-)

We recently stayed at Tallebudgera Creek Tourist Park for 3 nights. Great location and the amenities were super clean.
We loved our walk up to Burleigh Heads national park which was just across the road and spending time by the lake

Will definitely be back one day when we head back up to Queenslands

We have been staying at Tally for the past 10 yrs and love it, we stay on a waterfront site and spending the whole day sitting by the water is amazing, the shop and food van in the park is a extra bonus, and the adults only pool is fantastic Staff are always very friendly and helpful, love our yearly camping trip to Tally with our family and friends. 😁
Probably weren’t the easiest to deal with and loud traffic so it’s a no from me.

Unfortunately our stay didn’t feel like a true camping experience, as the constant noise from the nearby main road made it difficult to relax. Communication from staff was poor, and one receptionist came across as quite passive aggressive. The enforcement of rules felt excessive, with repeated interruptions and requests. Do not recommend.
Such an amazing location surrounded by safe swimming areas beaches and the magnificent Burleigh heads rainforest walk, never fails to deliver

We stay at the park everytime we are in QLD. Awesome spot, great park, friendly staff. Minutes away from waterways, beach, walks and cafes and surf clubs.
Just stay here, you won’t regret it.
We have grand kids & Tallebudgera Tourist park ticks every box for the kids to swim ,walk the beaches or close meals close to the park.
Loved the Site a great area very clean and well kept will be going again for sure

Loved our stay at Tullebugerra, can't rate rooms as we were in our own self contained Caravan. we have booked again for August this year. Facility's are great, bus close and Surf club all walking distance, looking forward to our return trip in a few weeks 🙂

Been caravanning for twelve years & found this gem of a park. Located on the beautiful Tallebudgera Creek & a short distance to the beach, this park has a lot to offer. Friendly staff, spacious sites, clean amenities & direct access to the river for your enjoyment. Will definitely be back!
Always a great place to stay.
We stayed in our caravan on powered site a few times now ammenities clean and staff are always helpful and friendly

The cabins need some $$$ spent on them - really showing their age. For the $ I expect better condition - fist holes in walls, damage on doors and bbq not working. Floors could be cleaned better and shower needs a good mould clean.  As always, the pool facilities was great.  The food van was a plus - but one of my battered fish was raw!  The kiosk is great addition - has everything!  Customer service at reception is average - wouldn’t even allow 1hr later check out (but would accept it if we paid $140 more).  We’ve been here many times but will try more modern facilities until they upgrade - not worth the $$

Disappointed by the Management

We’ve loved staying at Tallebudgera Caravan Park this was our third visit recently but unfortunately, our experience this time was ruined by how we were treated by the manager, Kate.

Every other staff member has gone above and beyond to help us, but Kate was dismissive and rude from the start. When we struggled to find a suitable site, she showed no interest in helping. My partner quietly expressed frustration (not directed at her), and from that moment, the situation escalated unnecessarily.

We’ve travelled full-time for a long time and have never been spoken to or treated like that before. It was embarrassing and uncomfortable, especially with other guests present. We felt pressured into a site we weren’t happy with, but thankfully, other staff stepped in later and helped us move. Ironically, the site we ended up on was one we asked about earlier, but Kate had brushed it off.

It’s disappointing when one person’s attitude affects the entire experience, especially in a place we’ve loved and spent a lot of money at. Sadly, it’s made us reconsider returning.

Just spent 9 nights there in our caravan. Great place to stay, with very good amenities, nice swimming pools and very friendly staff. Probably ridiculously busy in school holidays but if that’s not your thing, don’t go there at that time of year. We loved it and will be back!!
5 nights camping here. Good location for experiencing the area. Lovely clean facilities. Adults and kiddies pool, and well stocked shop. All in all an excellent site.
Excellent location with close proximity to the beach and creek. Great for the kids.

Great location at the southern end of the GC. A family oriented area with a creek for safe swimming. The park staff are very attentive and welcoming. However I feel the cabins need a bit of TLC and a bathroom upgrade. There also needs to be a modernised process for allowing visitor vehicles in.

We recently stayed on a premium site for 3 nights right in front of the heated swimming pool.
The staff were fabulous and willing to help with anything as well as stop and have a chat with you about your travels.
A very friendly park great for all travellers (singles, couples & families) as there is so much to do and so close to the hustle & bustle of the Gold Coast but far enough away to feel relaxed if that is your choice of holiday.
Will definitely be coming back to spoil ourselves on a water front site!!
So close to the rock pool, beach and break wall.
Can’t forget about the friendly kookaburras!!

We stayed for a weekend with family this is a great location and the staff were lovely but cabins are not up to standard the cabin had vertical blinds which had blinds missing in our bedroom which was very annoying when you like to sleep in on holiday, the light didn’t work in toilet and the emails about not having any extra guests when I booked a two bedroom supreme cabin is very unreasonable and the emails about the crime within the park is not good some security and TLC to cabins would be very good as the price is expensive. I actually responded to the email from Tallebudgera creek tourist park when asked did I enjoy my stay but after I responded with these issues I didn’t get a reply which I would classify as bad service

We recently spent three nights camping at Tallebudgera Creek Tourist Park and absolutely loved it! The atmosphere is relaxed and welcoming, making it perfect for all types of travellers—whether you’re going solo, with a group, or as a family.

The amenities were spotless and well-maintained throughout our stay. We really appreciated the adults-only pool for some quiet downtime, as well as the separate pool with slides that kept the kids entertained. The lawns and gardens were beautifully kept, adding to the peaceful vibe of the park.

The location is unbeatable—just a short drive to local shopping, centres, a walk to cafes, the beach, and of course the stunning creek, which is perfect for swimming or water activities. The campsites themselves are a generous size, giving you plenty of room to relax.

The staff were a real highlight—super friendly, helpful, and always going above and beyond to ensure we had a great stay.

We loved it so much we’re looking to go back again next month!

We stayed with a family group across a couple of cabins and powered sites. My family was in a 2 bedroom cabin.
Good location on the creek and across from the Surf Club, however be prepared to get bombarded with emails and messages. Ones of note were that in advance of arrival the cabin may not be ready for the booked 2pm check in, and also that you still need to leave by 10am and aren’t able to stay to use the facilities. The others are about how much crime and theft there is through the park, but also that if you bring visitors in they need to pay. One of the reception staff was rude when we approached them to report that one of the lights wasn’t working and that there was no dishwashing liquid in the cabin (which is a bit concerning as we were then unsure if any of the dishes and cutlery in the cabin were even clean).
In summary, nice location, some staff have a negative attitude, overpriced and some pretty unreasonable terms.
Great location for our caravan in the very well run park.

Overpriced, security very eager to watch you set up your tent, but not do anything about the noise after 10pm. People drinking and smoking in the kitchen next to my site, called security and nothing was done.
One of the best beach. Amazing clear water. The creek is so much fun.

Talle was fantastic to stay at, we had a Premium Powered Site and this was fantastic, well maintained clean and tidy and easy to get caravan into. Amenities were always clean when we needed to use them. Staff was always friendly and very helpful, great place to stay. Adults only pool was perfect to sit and enjoy, but if you have kids the family pool is awesome to spend the day sitting around. Food van supplies exceptional food and also the Wood Fired Pizzas were great.  Also the selection of ice-blocks to choose from was exceptional from the convenience store on site. The Surf Club across the road with exceptional food. Will definitely be back 😊👍🏻
Fantastic place, well looked after and maintained, friendly staff, great facilities plenty for families to do.

Well I have not been to this park for many years, but wow I would not recommend. I have just called the reception to make a booking for 2 weeks in may  and I have never experienced a reaction so disgusting from somebody that works in a reception in my life and I believe she was the manager that was what she said herself, and I called back to confirm. The name was Kate. Well I I am currently waiting for the Gold Coast council to get back to me. Hopefully they can get a record of conversation to actually hear the way I was spoken to on the way that stuff actually treat people and I am looking to make a complaint If this could be a zero star review, it really would be

This was the first time my family and I stayed here. We absolutely loved it!!
We stayed on the Eastern end of the park next to the highway. While we were concerned with the noise of traffic - it didn’t bother us in the slightest! We also had quite a generous sized lot which was perfect!
We also felt this was the better side of the park as we were close to the pool and amenities. The pool is awesome!
Staff were so lovely! It was good to see heaps of staff constantly patrolling the park and making sure all the bins weren’t overflowing.
There is a kiosk that has everything you could need if you forget something!

Also some tips:
- Smaller washing machines cost $5, larger machine cost $6 and the dryer was $1 for every 8 minutes. $4 did the trick each time which included towels.
The Kiosk will also provide $20 in gold coins if you forget / don’t have enough cash which came in so handy! Just note they are only gold coin machines for your trip!

- The amenities were clean and tidy. They were maintained by staff frequently throughout the day / evening.
- The parents bathroom is awesome too. However, I do wish there was one on the amenities side we were using. That would be my only feedback.

You’re not far from Pacific Fair for a shopping day! And Coles etc. is all on the along the GoldCoast Highway only a few minutes drive away.

Would 100% recommend spending time here! And I look forward to planning another trip here in the future!
great place, perfectly located and the staff were always super friendly. We can definitely recommend it.
One of my fav places in ask the world!
This park is fantastic, beautiful location, great staff and always plenty to do.

Great place to kick back and relax. 2nd time here, 1st time camping,  this time in a delux cabin . 20 minutes to Pacific Fair and Surfers paradise and 40 minutes to Harbour Town. Burleigh Heads surf club 5 minutes drive away. Park has a good pool, and Kart hire for the kids. Amenities are always clean, and good showers situated on Tallebudgera Creek with a netted area for swimming and a 10 minute walk to the beach.  I have no hesitation in recommending this park and will return again

Der Campingplatz liegt ca 5-10min vom Strand entfernt. An für sich ein guter Campingplatz aber bei uns waren die Toiletten komplett verdreckt oder die Türen konnte man nicht mehr schließen. Von 6 Türen waren 4 kaputt und die anderen zwei waren stark verschmutzt, ebenso die duschen. Für so viel Geld erwarte ich definitiv Sauberkeit. Die Mitarbeiter sind freundlich und hilfsbereit aber die Hygiene lässt nur 1 Stern zu, leider!
Fantastic park we had our caravan on a site near the creek. Great location beautiful area. Thanks.

Absolutely fabulous place to stay!
Tallebudgera Creek Tourist park is not like your typical caravan park. It is located in the best spot on the Gold Coast with everything you need from a short walk to the surf beach, to access to the creek where you can have a swim, float around or even do a bit of stand up paddle boarding.
The park is extremely well maintained by super friendly park staff. The amenities are always spotless. The kiosk is great with an ice creamery, essentials & even alcohol if you run out.
If you don’t want to go to the beach or creek, there are 3 pools located within the park including an adults only pool.
We absolutely love this place and it’s why we choose “Talle” as our home away from home for our holidays.

We have stayed at many places within Queensland and NSW but we always come back to Tallebudgera on a regular basis, we love the location, care and welcoming attitudes from all the staff and clean and tidy amenities.

The location is absolutely fantastic and the park itself is very clean. The staff were all very friendly and accommodating. The only issue with our cabin were the tiles being old and broken and the grout coming away. This caused a cut foot to a family member upon our first arrival. Might be worth replacing these or fixing them up to avoid further injuries.

You pay for the location here, but thankfully we had a free 2 night stay from purchasing our Urban Caravan with Hinterland Carvans.
The park is very clean. We had a powered slab site (341), up the back of the park and it was very quiet, and secluded between the 2 permanent sites.
Walk to the beach was only a 5 minute walk.
Staff were friendly, and the online check in process was very easy.

Fab place to stay, so many things to do.
Very clean amenities, 2 super pools including
adults only and so close to the beach. Food trucks and onsite shop/cafe is really handy.
Definitely going back.

We booked for 3 nights, unfortunately upon arrival the site was under construction. No working water slides, wifi or camp kitchen. In the end we left after one night - and paid for two. The location is great - service and information before booking should be improved
Our favourite place to visit on the Gold Coast. 3rd time this year!!!!
Ok caravan park. Facilities were about 4/5. The place just seemed to lack the feel of good beach camping caravan park.
Going through some upgrades at the moment but still a great spot
Renovations bit noisy but the place is great for camping.

Unpowered site per night was 70$ at no peak time.
Toilets and showers are a bit far away from the site and need to be renovated. They looked clean in general but in the evening there were some cockroaches in the sink.
All in all too expensive for what you get on the unpowered site.

We had a great time here. Lovely family friendly place. It was a nice chilled vibe.
The adult only pool was a bonus!!
Nice big and clean bathrooms too.
The only downside was the lack of a kitchen area. They only have bbqs, a kettle and a toaster

The perfect park in the perfect location!
We love staying at Tallebudgera tourist park whether we stay in a cabin or our caravan, we are never disappointed. The park is clean, the amenities are clean, the staff are always friendly.
Our kids love the freedom and safety we feel at Tallebudgera tourist park. This has become a yearly tradition for our family and friends and we wouldn’t change a thing!
Great place, have been coming here for years
Amazing place to stay.  Location is perfect and our cabin was perfect, right on the beach.  Great facilities and the team bent over backwards to help us.  Coming back for sure.
We absolutely love this park 🌞
Very good stayed 2 weeks and had a creekside cabin pool good pizza good and yummmy would recommend

Fantastic spot to camp, with amazing facilities for water park, Tennis, playgrounds, kitchen and bathroom/shower facilities. Great for a getaway with young families within close proximity to beaches and still creeks which are perfect for stand-up paddle boarding.
Love our yearly break at Talle creek. Extra large sites available for big set ups. Great facilities. The location is the best. So central and public transport at the front of park.
Wow!, room is an understatement, enjoyed our stay, will most definitely be back

Paid for day pass parking whilst visiting friends staying! Facilities clean and serviced. Kiosk has what you need but have to pay for it! Right on creek and creek is clear and beautiful. Fishing and watersport an option. Long stays only though! Tried to get one night ( not an option) 😞

The best beach all around the world. White sand, nice beach and close ocean if you want surfing. You can try paddleboard, windsurfing or just swimming
Plenty of toilets, picnic spots and restaurants. The only problem is parking. Even at 7 in the morning impossible to find a parking spot.
Went to visit family and friends lovely clean park great amenities
Très belle plage.

Stayed in caravan park. Beautiful location with great access to creek, beach, coastal walks, and Burleigh National Park.
Facilities were well maintained and the park was clean and well kept. Surprisingly quiet given location, and overnight security over the weekend ensured quiet nights.
Reception staff were friendly and very accommodating when we requested to extend our stay. We will be back.

Stayed in a caravan at the park for almost a week, was originally for 2 nights, we keeped extending our stay.
Loved it!
only thing we would suggest, pool need to close later. 6pm is a bit early.
Overpriced $234 for 3 night and parked us on a corner with no concrete pad so we felt out of place, over packed with kids, toilets are filthy and nothing to do here
A great place to stay for families. Loved the ensuite option for camping as well!

Fabulous river-front caravan park. We enjoyed our stay. Swimming in the creek, walks to Canteen Custard for our morning caffeine hit and a delicious muffin. The kids played for hours on the sand banks of Talkebudgera creek. This has been one of our favourite caravan park stays on our whole 12 month lap of Australia. Thank you!

We only stayed at the park for 2 nights and the weather wasn't very good. We still enjoyed dinner both nights, one at the food van on site and one at the Surf Club across the road. The amenities were were clean and well maintained . Access to the river and beach was great , a lovely walk in between rain showers.Entertainment for families is great with pools, playgrounds and trikes to ride. We enjoyed our stay and will be back again.

Great destination to.stay with a camper trailer or caravan.

Clean facilities.
Friendly staff.
Had a week in our van at the big T caught a fish, walked to The surf clubs for dinner and drinks had a great week
Very pleased with our 2 night stay. Park was bigger than we expected but it was still very peaceful. Facilities were clean and tidy.

We have stayed here multiple times and absolutely love it. The park has the perfect mix of being in nature but having everything at your fingertips. We love the walk to Burleigh, fishing in the creek, the kids play endlessly in the river and it’s easy to walk across to the Surf Club for dinner. The pool is fun for the kids, easy to check in and out, the park has great holiday activities.
Thanks for having us!
We love Talle caravan park! The sites are big and well kept. The location doesn't get much better! And the staff are always friendly. Highly recommend!
Had a nice few days there, and I should have stayed longer. We didn't make it to the pool as to many other things to do
It was so fun I really enjoy it

Our family of 5 had a wonderful stay and would highly recommend to others. The 2bdrm superior cabin was great had everything we needed was very clean and we loved the balcony high up and even used the BBQ on it when family members came for a day visit. The kids loved the heated swimming pool and the slide also right on the water another great bonus.

We had a lovely time.  The staff were extremely friendly and the location is magnificent.  The park was very full but clean, everything was at our fingertips, the two pools and beach access kept the kids busy.  Great park!
Best tourist Park I’ve stayed in. Definitely going back again and again!
Quiet and peaceful during the week. Gets very busy on the weekends but we have enjoyed it very much.
Hidden gem

Stayed at this park as part of a group of school aged students doing some training in Outdoor Recreation.  As part of this we travel to many parts of Queensland and stay in a wide variety of accommodation.  Facilities here are the best we've seen, despite being on a busy highway we felt secure and the staff couldn't have been more accommodating.   Location gives easy access to beaches and Burleigh Headland.  Perfect for us, will be back!

We recently stayed in the two bedroom apartment for a few days and it was great! It rained the entire time we were there, however the accommodation was large enough to spend time inside and have visitors come in from the rain. Cannot wait for our next holiday, we’ll be definitely booking here again!
Stayed for 3 nights amazing place easy check in and out amazing staff right on a amazing location can’t wait to come back all the way from rural vic
Great camping spot hope to go back
Great spot! Friendly service. If you get the chance to stay here, consider yourself lucky. It's a beautiful spot !

We recently stayed at Tallebudgera Creek tourist park in our own caravan on a powered site. The organisation and communication from the park team was better than excellent!! Being given the gate code and our site number before arrival was awesome and made arrival super quick and easy. The park was clean and well looked after and even the addition of a ‘kid free’ pool was just perfect for us! I cant comment on facilities as we didnt have to use them. I would definitely stay again, but maybe not on a public holiday!! Be good to secure a water front site next time!

What a great place to stay, we had the three-bedroom unit on the beach front. Great location, easy walk around to the main beach, fish in the creek, paddle board, plenty of room and well serviced with all requirements. Cool heated swimming pools as well.

This has got to be one of the best parks we have stayed at.The check in process is so easy as they contact you the day prior to arrival and provide you with a gate access code and your site number.
The amenities were cleaned regularly and were spotless.The onsite cafe is available daily for snacks and coffees etc.There’s a regular pizza van which provided amazing wood oven pizzas.
Best of all is the location.Right on the creek.With a short walk to the surf life club and eateries.Shops are a short drive/ walk away.I would highly recommend this holiday resort.
Safe, kids friendly park and plenty of space to have a bbq and to pitch up our tents. Beach is nice

Yes, we stayed here in our Caravan, on a PREMIUM POWERED SITE.. 👋 Hi Scott, I didn't want to upset the KPI's mate 😄

Be so great if it was Pet Friendly! 👋🎤 😆

The location was lovely and we had a great time with our friends.
We have camped at many place over the years but never have we come across such rude and unaccommodating reception staff.
We will not return nor will we be recommending.
Such a great location.

Lucky enough to just spend 3 nights at this beautiful peice of paradise. All I can say is AMAZING.kids loved it. Location perfect staff perfect,we stayed in a 3bedroom waterfront cabin, very clean and tidy has everything you need and more. Definately worth the money.even had a fire show for everyone right in front of our cabin, Birds Eye view.will certainly be back. Thanks again for making our stay so wonderful.

School holidays. We took our granddaughter for 4 nights, and she loved it. Great pre-check in, so there were no queues lined up for miles. There was a food truck, open most nights for meals, great food & an average price. Unfortunately, the shower/toilet blocks were used by hundreds of people & not everyone supervised their kids, so they required cleaning more often, as they were always in a disgusting state.

We spend 3 months every Winter at Tallebudgera Creek Tourist Park in our caravan. We always take an en suite site. There are a number of special features about this Park that make it elite:
1. It is bounded by Tallebudgera Creek, which at this point is really a sandy river, and the Gold Coast Highway, which makes it very accessible.
The Park has pedestrian and bicycle access to Palm Beach walkway via a pathway under Gold Coast Hwy, so no traffic to negotiate.
2. The Staff are amazingly helpful and create a beautiful culture in the Park. The facilities are impeccable.
3. Tally is a very social place where long term friendships are made.
A great park facilities are really good...very busy park during peak periods but close to everything.

Not a bad spot. Very large park.
Pool is great for the kids although the “baby/toddler” pool was freezing.
Easy access to beach/creek swimming & close enough to supermarket etc.
customer service at front reception could do with improvement.
Beautiful beach, nice food and coffee, nice pool area, very clean amenities.

A fantastic stay at the Tallebudgera Creek Tourist Park. This is in a great location for beach access, coffee and restaurants. It has the best Caravan Site check-in in the country. They send you your gate code, site number via email before arrival and you go straight in and set up. When you are done, just wander down to the office and show your ID and you're done. Makes for a 30 second check-in process. The staff of very friendly and accommodating. We will definitely return.

The location, ensuite, ground facilities (pools, river secure area and shop) and of course the staff, were all fantastic. There's no need to leavecthevoark, however if you do, there's plenty to see and do near by. I recommend this park for couples and families alike.

We were lucky enough to stay at Tally for 2.5 weeks. It’s a good location with young kids. The creek is a lovely place to play and explore, and the pool is very gently heated at this time of year. Sites are decent sized and good toilets and showers. The camp kitchen needs to be cleaned a little more regularly and fridges could do with an upgrade, although I think most people carry their own now so not much of a problem for most. We did lose various bits of food to the homeless people who come through checking the fridges; we met and spoke to three people doing this so there must be quite a few as we only spent a couple hours at a day at the camp kitchen. We also lost some pool toys to people grabbing them off our site which is a bit unfortunate. Overall though, just need to be aware of keeping your stuff secure and you’ll have a good stay.

I do want to mention that the people who run Tally really do go above and beyond. We were on a site while our house roof had some repairs, and the worst happened and our house ended up damaged and contaminated with asbestos. I was incredibly lucky that the lovely people at Tally extended our stay over the Gold Coast show long weekend, even though they were very full. Their kindness and compassion made an incredibly difficult time for our family much easier. Grateful to be home now but will keep Tally in mind for a future relaxing holiday with the family.
A great park with good facilities, good sized sites and friendly staff all located close to a beautiful beach. Highly recommend a stay at this lovely park, its well worth a stay.

The van sites are extremely large which is amazing. There are strict rules for quiet times during the evening and I noticed a security presence which is encouraging in this day and age. The facilities are well kept, the staff fantastic and the location brilliant. 5 stars.
Great caravan park. Well kept and tidy. Good sites. Staff pleasant and helpful. Highly recommended

Our powered tent site was right next to the amenities in the middle of the park, which was convenient.
There wasn't a smell, and they kept it quite clean.
The only thing is that the trees were flowering so we had some bird poo and flowers stuck everywhere, but the shade was good.
It's a bit unlucky, but it's spring so we sucked it up.
This is probably the most chilled site on the southern Gold Coast.
Would def stay again. 💯

Absolutely amazing place
Tallebudgerra never disappoints

Tallebudgera Creek Tourist Park is a fantastic joint and has something for all ages. my first stay was a very pleasant experience and will be looking at making return trips a tradition.
all facilities are clean and tidy and the pool is perfect for relaxing near by.
location speaks for itself.. famous Burleigh beach and Palm Beach within walking distance.

I loved staying here and will definitely be returning.

Great location
Friendly staff
Good price for powered concrete site, site was flat, facilities were good - hot showers and plenty of them
Family bathroom always appreciated for getting the kiddos bathed/showered.
Playground could do with a reno but otherwise lovely campsite, we didn't use the pool but the water slides looked fun!

We love the Tallebudgera caravan and tourist park. The location is amazing with the inlet or surf, cafes and wonderful walks.
The amenities are clean and well maintained.
The highlight is the staff who are fantastic. They are happy and go out of their way to be helpful. I would highly recommend this place.
Loved our stay. Everything you need. Very comfortable. Very clean. Best view of the water and magic sunsets.

Worst camping experience in a high class tourist Park. Some sites should not even be given out, but I guess they are after the money. Next to a very noisy busy road, in a full soft mud. Staff are definitely smiley, but uncooperative. I would never come back here.

Great park and van site
Awesome clean facilities
Excellent friendly staff

We were very happy with our accommodation at Tallebudgera camping ground. Great size big shower great outlook! Really very happy with our accommodations. Would definitely book there again. Lovely staff very helpful.
Really loved this caravan park. Perfect location right on inlet and across the road from beach. A stunning walk around to Burleigh Heads. Amenities were excellent and heated swimming pools.
Stayed last weekend 3 days . Had a wonderful stay .amenities where so clean .park kept wonderful .great place to vist n bring the family .will be staying again . Thank u so much to the staff .
Had a fantastic 4 night stay. Staff were very friendly. All in all a great place to enjoy a holiday. Thank you.
I've been treated amazingly and the staff has helped me alot this is one best places to go if u want a fun holiday and recieve absolute respect and kindness

Everything about this park is 5 star. From the start with check in, friendly efficient staff,  the park grounds are so clean, as was the pool, and the ensuite we booked was spotless, even included hand towel and bath mat which I didn’t expect. Such a great spot with the estuary and beach at your doorstep,  a surf club across the road, a short walk over the bridge and thru Burleigh hill gets you to Burleigh and into the cafes. We love it here and will keep coming back. Thankyou team…keep up the great work!

Quality Caravan Park for the Gold Coast. Perfect location with well kept common areas. Staff are very friendly and willing to assist with anything.

Location is perfect and close to very nice resturants and pubs. we went to the Collective and had a great time with a large menu to pick from.

The caravan powered spots are clean and tidy, easy to park on. we have stayed there a few times and in different spots, i.e. powered with ensutie and powered.

Keep up the great work and we will be back for sure.

K&J
Great located park, friendly staff & happy they have clamped down on kids riding scooters n bikes after dark, well done 👏
Wonderful park. Very clean amenities and grounds. Staff friendly, respectful, and knowledgeable. Good size sites. Beautiful location. Will definitely return.
Such a great location with excellent service and amenities. Will be back for sure.

We really enjoyed our 16 nights on a powered slab caravan site across from the water front sites, it was very spacious.  The reception girls were so lovely & helpful right from check in. Amenities kept clean. Had well stocked kiosk with coffees. Food van on site wed- sun, & whilst it’s busy it still feels like you have your own space. Nice walking path straight to beach. Highly recommend.
Fantastic caravan park in a fantastic location- extremely family friendly. Every thing is spotlessly clean and the staff are, professional,friendly and helpful.

Friendly and accommodating staff, very clean facilities, the amenities were well kept and running great, there's a handy kiosk onsite and food truck food was great. Good access to Tally Creek and the swimming area. Loved that the fresh seafood van came around the park. We didn't ever need to leave the park. Definitely coming back with our group of friends again.

What a location! It was heaven. We loved our stay and spent many happy moments at the creek walking and swimming. The camp ground was absolutely packed being school holidays but we had space and the park activities were fun. The communication from team Tally was excellent and we would definitely come back.

Great camping trip .  Location fantastic especially with keen fishing teenagers.  Amenities always clean and plenty there. Impressed with the food truck at the park great size meals very enjoyable.  Thanks for great experience
All staff polite and accommodating. Grounds and ammenities always clean and tidy. Will be back👏👏

We would like to thank Team Tally for such a great stay. All staff are very friendly and helpful. We had to extend a couple of times and nothing was any issue and they did ever thing in their power to keep us on the same site. Amenities kept very clean and new dump points. Tanya & Tim are great managers and it is a credit to them they way they run their team.
Thank you everyone Sharon & Peter
Nice well maintained CP, clean amenities, great location on the creek. Nice  powered sites and wide paths to manoeuvre a caravan around the park .

We have been coming to Tallebudgera for over 15 years. Firstly, in a tent with our 4 sons who still rate these holidays as their best (and we have travelled to o/s locations).  Over the years we have also booked cabins for extended family get togethers. Perfect location. Recently we have started to take a caravan down on spare weekends.  The fishing spot is perfect in the late afternoons. Facilities have been looked after much better since new management and the staff in office very friendly and helpful.
Mel
Great spot on the waterfront - a variety of scenic walks to choose from

What a great caravan park! We have stayed at Tallebudgera three times over the last three years and have booked again for 2025.
The location is great, the Staff extremely friendly and helpful, the cafe has great coffee and the Ensuite site we had was great and really well presented and was even serviced over our two weekly stay! We highly recommend Tallebudgera Creek Tourist park!

Camping is my favourite thing to do and Talle has become my favourite holiday park to stay at!
The location cannot be beaten and the parks facilities & friendly staff create an amazing holiday vibe.
Even though the Park has everything you would need for a great holiday, the park itself if surrounded by natures paradise.
Can’t wait to return and holiday here again.

Great caravan park in a good location However there are works going on at the moment. We paid for premium sites and find ourselves in a construction zone with a constant noise and smell of resin. We have been told at the office that these works are going on for possibly months. We were only notified of these works one hour prior to arrival. I suggest not staying here until the works are complete. No peace and quiet, day or night. Pity!             PS The manager came and spoke with me after my review and said the timing of the works was out of his control. The works did stop during the weekend but resumed again on Monday morning for the daytime only.

Enjoyed a fantastic stay last weekend and WOW what a location on beautiful Tallebudgera Creek with lovely waterfront and National park headland walks, BBQs, swimming, biking options and great couple of pools at caravan park…recommend site 284A plenty of room and near pool (but not too close) and clean amenities.  Big park but surprisingly quiet and bus stop on your doorstep to head north Burleigh, Broadbeach, Surfers etc out south to airport, Kirra & Coolangatta… oh and there’s theTalle Surf Club and cafe across the road too… highly recommend staying here

Tallebudgera Tourist Park has been our happy place for many, many years now. Recently the park has been undertaking some major upgrades to their underground services and a month ago we were disappointed to be informed that planned works had been extended and our booked waterfront site unfortunately wasn’t going to be available. We accepted their predicament and took a site in suburbia. Sensing our site disappointment when we arrived, Tanya worked some magic and was able to make a waterfront site available for the duration of our stay. Great service and customer care is always forthcoming from all of the happy staff here

We booked this spot a year ago  and paid a deposit of $150. We changed dates once with months of notice. Recently, we had to change dates again due to some unforeseen circumstances. The management refused to change dates or refund our deposit. They would not give us a credit either and forced us to cancel our booking. Please be aware of their cancellation policy before you pay a deposit. Quite frankly I was disappointed at their lack of empathy and greed for money.

The worst experience I’ve had in all my holidays. Customer service is absolutely appalling. The facilities (which were $400 a night) had multiple issues including the toilet not flushing, pipes clunking all night, broken windows, doors not locking. I understand things break but when these issues were raised with management, they didn’t revolve anything for 4 days, of our 5 day stay, despite us following up with them multiple times.

The manager was more worried about how these facility issues inconvenient him. He gaslit us and refused to acknowledge any of the impact these issues had on our stay. You couldn’t pay me to stay here again. I strongly recommend staying elsewhere if you want an enjoyable holiday.

Big machine making a noise like a jet engine working in the caravan park10 meters from my camp site going all the sleepless night. I complained and received this response....regarding the work....Extract:-
"For your booking we sent you advisory emails on 11th April and on the 27th April and a reminder text yesterday morning referencing the emails that had been sent.

There will be no compensation offered as you have decided to stay despite the email notification".

I might add that the emails did not say that there would be this machine making this noise all night.....
Reading between the lines is not necessary as the message is very plain....WE WILL DO WHAT WE WANT WHEN WE WANT.
I could have used more blunt language but.......
Great park ,friendly staff,every day of our stay Tim and his ground staff made us laugh we will definitely be back
The best holiday park ever!!! I spend as much time here as possible.  The best location and the best staff!!
All good here. Clean. Well organised. Good location. On the water. I stayed in bunk house cabins. Very good with shared facilities. Bathroom in the block. Camp kitchen out the front. A great stay.

Fantastic place to stay. We've stayed at many caravan parks over 20 years but we always come back to Tallebudgera. We have had generations come to this park , now my grandchildren are coming. The bathrooms and laundry facilities are by far the best in the camping world. Tallebudgera has the best of both worlds, with the wonderful creek and the beaches under the bridge, with cafes and clubs within walking distance, you have everything you need for a relaxing holiday. Thanks for our great memories staff at Tally, our second home.
We have been many times, this place is just wonderful for families!!
My daughter and family went there camping and they loved it we went for a day and ut was a great place

This is one of the best parks we've ever visited - great staff, great facilities and a fantastic location within walking distancee of the beach/swimming area and a cafe. Thoroughly recommended for both families and couples. We've been there multiple times and are looking forward to returning soon.

We had a camper trailer two rows back from the water and it was nice but no views.
It’s very noisy on the water side because of the youth centre using it to have 30 or so kids every day doing water sports.
The surf life saving people also do there training there with surf boats.
All good

Best park on the coast. NO DOGS.
Staff friendly and helpful.
We spent 2 weeks at Tally, the cost, location, staff and facilities were amazing!! We could not fault our stay at all... thanks Tally Team.. xx

The park facilities are excellent with two swimming pools. We enjoyed the kid free pool but also loved having our grandkids visit and enjoy the kids pool area too.

The camp kitchens have sinks, fridges and free electric bbqs.

Great Gold Coast location that has food options at the park plus a cafe and Tally Surf Club across the road. There is excellent walking tracks to the beach, Burleigh and Palm Beach where you’ll find great swimming and fishing opportunities at the ocean or in Tally Creek.

The amenities are always very clean and tidy plus caravan sites are a great size!

The park staff go out of their way to ensure you have a memorable stay weather they are the office staff, cleaners or grounds staff.

We are looking forward to staying again soon 😀

What a great experience. From the time of booking until checkout our contact with staff was brilliant. We had a perfect spot right on river front and near bike walking track. Amenities were clean, visitors all friendly. Just wished we could have chilled out here longer. I would highly recommend this park for all families as  perfect for all ages.

This is our home away from home. The grounds are always everything you want in a tourist park, but it’s the location that makes this place magical. Onsite food…. The best burger on the coast by far! Then it’s only a short stroll to the beach, amazing hikes and world class quality restaurants.
No matter if you’re a family or a couple having a cheeky weekend away, this is definitely the park for you.

We've stayed here a number of times now and it has always been a great experience. The facilities are always clean, the staff are friendly and the shop and the food truck are great too. Highly recommend if you are a family looking for a great holiday destination.

We have been coming here for 7 years now and have never had a bad experience. Staff are professional and courteous.

The grounds are very well maintained, the bathrooms are kept clean and who doesn't love a hot shower .

Plenty for kids of all ages to do all around park , with playground, swimming pools that have water slides and water toys for younger kids. A nice quite pool for adults who like a slower pace.

Great food van and kiosk (try the Cruffins) for those small grocery items etc you might run out off

We think it is the best on the coast for us!
Amenities were clean, laundry facilities were excellent. Pool was very nice and warm. The food truck was an awesome idea that saved our lunch on the first day. Five stars would come back. :)

Recently visited Tallebudgera Creek Tourist Park and stayed in one of the deluxe 2 bed cabins. The accomodation was immaculate, comfy and very convenient.
The park and staff were great - everyone is friendly and there’s a small shop on site for coffee/ice creams and  basic needs (with two major centres a short drive away).
You’re nearby so many beautiful spots for swimming and exploring. Highly recommend and hope to be back again one day.

We had a wonderful stay at this park.
It is in the best site on the Gold Coast
Great staff.
Perfect spot for families, cabins were a bit outdated but still serviceable. Book early! It's super popular.
