Worst customer service on the face of the earth. I told them when we arrived I have autism and sensory processing disorder and asked for a quiet spot. They put us directly opposite the loudest works I’ve ever heard without any warning that it would be going on. If you ask to extend your stay they won’t bother managing their booking system properly and will make you move sites and pack everything just for an extra night even though half the pack is empty. Refusing to refund even though the situation is completely unliveable and deafening. Absolutely will not be back. Ever.

Other than the location I am disappointed in the park.  In the many years (most of my life) we as a family have been going there and this year would have to be the worst. Everything was dirty feeling with mould in the shower area, our site being a dirt patch which has been like it for years even though we have spoken to them about it.  Their rebooking is not a very nice experience. Like other reviews of people not happy I have read I would agree with them.  Making excuses for the way the park is, is not good enough and the issues we saw have been going on for years.  Toilet and shower doors not shutting properly and the list goes on.  Don’t say they are working on it when it has been an issue for so long.  The money we pay to spend 2 weeks down there is ridiculous for the conditions we are out in.

If I could give this park a negative rating I would.

This place destroys the happiness in a holiday!

Previous reviews have commented on the management staff being rude and condescending. We experienced similar along with a lack of respect and concern for their guests, absolute arrogance and stonewalling.

Our “deluxe suite” had a list of maintenance issues discovered when we first arrived. Doors and windows which couldn’t be closed, a worry worsened by heavy rain, grey water bubbling up from the drain behind the deluxe unit next door and spilling over on to the lawn, the one and only toilet not flushing during our stay, clunking plumbing that disturbed our nights every 10-15 minutes, the kitchen tap that stopped working for a time, an incredibly noisy expellair in the bathroom which we were too scared to use, blinds that were faulty & inadequate, front door keys that were tenuous. And to top it off, without warning or discussion, being told we couldn’t use the one and only toilet. Very upsetting for a young child experiencing gastric problems. Difficult and disturbing.

After identifying the many issues in the Deluxe cabin, we were then subjected to maintenance staff interrupting our days coming and going without warning. It was an inconvenience, interrupting children’s sleep and quiet times. The maintenance problems meant several visits, some issues remaining unresolved, others required continued interruptions over the following days. There was no discussion as to the possible impact on us, no consideration to our needs at all.

Meeting with the duty manager, as with everything else, was absolutely frustrating.  We experienced Stonewalling and Deflection.

This park stay has been an absolutely appalling experience. And yes, I fully expect the GM’s response to be the same as the duty manager’s. A complete denial of any responsibility, a disdainful attitude towards guests, and more deflection. A totally unacceptable level of customer service.

The thin veil which was our initial positive interaction with front line staff, and the park’s location, pales into total insignificance when balanced with the roughshod arrogance and totally unacceptable behaviours that we have experienced. I sure hope others don’t get to experience a holiday like this. In all my 70 years, this is one holiday venue I wished I’d never experienced, nor do I want to ever again. It has been totally unacceptable.

And now I fully expect to hear the accusations, the deflection, the denial and the excuses begin. It seems to me to be the pattern, the behaviours used, to intimidate.

Location is excellent, but that’s where it stops.

Management are easy to find, just walk to the kiosk by the main gate (but don’t go inside) Instead, bang your head against the nearest brick wall and then walk away…. You’ll feel better and get the same result.

Customer service is non-existent.  Customer interactions (as a tourism provider) are atrocious.

Doesn't get much better than here, stunning location. The creek is safe, wide & clean & the park is very well maintained. Great place to stay. We stayed here in our caravan & would definitely come back. In fact we've rebooked already

Good spot
But all the aboriginal stuff has been destroyed in local area quite sad
Great place, Chris from the grounds department is an absolute champion. Thanks legend.

Great, clean camp in great location. 2 stars because Kate was very rude and unfriendly, two times. Other crew does the great job.

EDIT:
Scott contacted me and apologized for the situation. Very professional way of approach to the customers!

Very urbanised caravan park. With many facilities and top rooms. Very clean and well maintained. The location is fantastic.
Maybe too packed and occasionally it takes a long time to get in or out.

We stayed in a 5 person waterfront cabin as a stay-cation and had a great time.  The cabin was a generous size, we have stayed in a few that were a bit pokey in the past at other places, but this one was a good size.  It was also very clean and had everything we needed including mozzie repellent and fly spray, which was needed.

If there was one recommendation I would make, would be to replace grill mesh sliding screens with plain mesh sheet screens to take in the beautiful view more.  Between the mesh screens, the vertical blinds and the horizontal balcony railing it's a bit distracting when looking out.  It's not a biggie, but would make it so much nicer!

We spent our time between the pool and the beach and it was enough to keep everyone occupied.

The check in and check out was smooth and friendly. We mentioned our toilet had been making noises during our stay and the staff wrote down notes straight away to have it tended to.

We fell in love with a resident hobbling lorikeet we named Pika who was very tame and came into our cabin for some grapes and pear.  The swans that came to visit each day were also a standout.

We didn't use the main park facilities or camp kitchen, with the camp kitchen being very simple I'm not sure if there were any ovens but we saw bbq's.  The playground the kids weren't all too keen on but were happy enough with the pool area.

A bit of privacy between the cabins would have been nice, but no biggie, there are plenty of people looking in from their daily walks anyway.

It was nice to be so close to the good restaurants in Burleigh and Palm Beach.  Would definitely recommend Frida Sol for some really tasty Mexican food in Palm Beach.  There was also a pizza truck that came at night, which we didn't try but were told was very good (and very convenient location).

Tallebudgera creek caravan Park would have to be the best family holiday destination I have been to in my lifetime so far, the facilities location and staff made the stay quite memorable,it was a hive of activity all day the kids were outdoors all day and never got bored

A bit of price gauging .
Great position.

Grounding, healing and so incredibly beautiful.
Nothing beats a clear day & visiting this incredible place.
So good. We go back every year.

Very dissapointed with our stay. The manager was so rude. He spoke down to us in a very threatening manner because we misunderstood his parking rules. Rather than speak to us rationally and come up with a solution he threatened to kick us,,2 grandparents 2 grandchilden and their parents, out of HIS park. Yet we had approx 10 or more young people outside our 'family room' drinking and being loud and he said nothing.
The 'family rooms' are more backpackers accomodation than for families. One toilet and shower to accomodate over 30 people is a joke. If you can bare the noise from the trailer folk the highway drag races outside the window and the fans which have 2 speeds..slow and off..will make sure your stay its not one bit restful.
I guess I could have tolerated it if the manager hadn't been so horrible and wrecked our holiday. Checked out and found somewhere a lot nicer.
Read less

Fantastic location. Very friendly staff in the park. Sadly, our booking for 3 carravan sites for our family was totally stuffed up, and we ended up in different parts of the park because despite being given the van sizes one of the sites was to small. This meant that we did not get to enjoy the long weekend as much as we had hoped.
We love our holidays at Talle ! So many memories and friends made.  Amazing location, great facilities and an awesome team of staff.

I was unimpressed to say the least. I called up to reserve a non powered site , i was told $65 for the night. Upon arrival i was told she accidentally put the wrong date down and that there were no non powered sites available. Of course that meant the price is now $119 for one night of sleeping in a tent. I payed the $119 out of frustration because i drove over an hour to get there. The camp site was completely surrounded by hundreds of people & loud music in all directions. No where to sit and have a quiet night and just over all not a nice place to relax. We packed our stuff and left within two hours of arriving. I would not recommend this to anyone and wish i kept my money.

Currently loving my stay Tally van park, real beach weather, so the best place to be with pools, creek and ocean, plenty of shade, team have the park looking fab, office girls are super helpful.  The food truck never disappoints.

Loved our stay at Talle ! While the rooms are quite dated, I can’t fault the location ! The team are wonderful and I have to give a shoutout to Emily for being so helpful. Can’t wait to come back next summer !
Love this place but parking is crazy, getting in early to secure a good spot is crucial, fair few parking tickets on windscreens. Beware.
Fantastic park

Nous sommes venues avec notre van sur un emplacement sans électricité, déjà nous étions placés à côté de la route, donc assez bruyant mais surtout ils nous ont pas prévenus que nous étions en dessous des arbres avec les oiseaux qui défèquent sur la voiture continuellement. Camping cher nous avons payés 100$ à deux, faites attentions où ils vous placent. Sinon la localisation du camping est idéal pour visiter les alentours

This tourist park has it all. This was my third December spent at Talle and it’s just getting better each time. The beach, the pools and the amenities are beyond amazing and the whole park is VERY well looked after. Clean and tidy always. The staff are wonderful. A tornado went through Christmas night … the whole park rallied together and helped eachother out. This shows the family atmosphere the park provides. The next day the park was cleaned in record time. Thankyou for making it feel like home away from home. It was so hard to leave after 3 weeks. But can not wait to get back there.

We have almost finished our stay at the park on and off over the last three months. Everything has exceeded expectation. The aftermath of the crazy storms that hit the area on Christmas evening were dealt with so promptly and efficiently by all the staff. Massive thumbs up to all the staff that are out battling the heat to keep the park looking amazing.

The friendliness and upbeat personalities of the staff is to be commended.

Any needs or requests we have had over the last couple of months have been handled professionally and always leading to a perfect outcome. We could not have asked for more.

The other visitors to the park have all been great. It is the best family friendly atmosphere and everyone has been very respectful.

I cannot recommend this park highly enough. A naturally beautiful area with so much to do and fabulous staff just tops it off perfectly!!

Thanks so much Team Tally!!!!

Terrible Terrible Terrible
Do yourself a favour and stay else where, extremely rude staff with massive Karen chip.
Overpriced, dirty, hidden fees and charges Not worth the money

Undate
I have been very sick for the past 2 days and because of a mix up on Tally  end we were forced to pack up in sweltering heat and I vomited 3 times today and now my wife just vomited .
It’s no doubt the the unclean conditions contributed to this sickness.

As I said do yourself a favour and stay anywhere els
I’ll give a further update tmw

Update
This is where the the lies really start. I got a very aggressive phone call from Casey  claiming to be the park Manager.
Now who is the Manager, Scott ,  Tim & Tanya or Casey?
Casey said the the booking was not quite right and I would need to pay a further exorbitant fee and she was charging my credit card , I explained that is theft and i would charge them personally.
I tried to explain that we had done everything correctly and the error must be on their end .

To summarise basically within a 20 minute period, we were told Pay up or leave.

We are experienced Caravaners and never have we been treated so disgustingly, no apologies
Tally is just a well oiled money grabbing business and clients come last.
I’m still so sick and will update tmw
Great park one of the 👌

It is a really nice and beautiful camping spot, in front of a creek. Really well organised, clean amenities, open kitchen with basic goods(fridge, microwave, kettle, no utensiles), spacious bathroom... they have a playground, a coffee shop, laundry, pools for everyone. My kids loved. We stayed in a camp site and they are really packed, not much space between the sites. Looking at the creek, on right side is a patrolled beach/creek but we went to left and there is a bay area and enclosed area as well. Be careful of your belongs because anyone can come inside from the creek/beach. Also if you are light sleeper and in a tent, there is a big road next to the camping and cars all day/night.
the place is nice and grate for young kids having waterslides and a massive pool also having much room to ride bikes and scooters the beds were a little bit dirty also having a crack on the mirror
Amazing Creek Tourist Park. Highly recommended

Very lovely place to visit. Everything is close or within walking distance. They have a kiosk and pizza van open nightly. Surf club across the road for breakfast, lunch, or dinner. Surf is across the road, or right in front of the tourist park is a great place for the kiddies to swim and parents to be able to relax. There are 2 pools available in the park as well.

Camped for the weekend with my daughter and granddaughter, campsite was nice and close to the facilities (which is important for a 6 year old). The pool was the highlight for my granddaughter and I reckon she probably went up and down it about 50 times. The camp ground was just across the road from Tallebudgera Creek and we popped over for a few hours on the Saturday for a swim and brunch.
What I liked about the campground was that it had everything we needed and we didn't have to use the car at all.
The toilets were clean and the staff were very friendly.
I highly recommend camping here. We will definitely be back.

Beautiful place and close to Creek to Sea, amazing! Walk to the beach, and Cafe is very enjoyable ☺️ The caravan park public toilets/showers need upgrades because the doors are broken and taps are loose, etc....

Nice park with good and clean amenities and activities for the kids. The further you are located away from the entrance the quieter it is. The front of the campground is right at the GC motorway. We've been there twice and enjoyed the proximity to the water.

Water front 5 stars
Don't have small kids so not sure about pool facilities, but I can assure you that water front at Tally is gods country
Hate having to pick up other people's litter.
Use bins please

Fabulous park with fabulous faculties and caring, friendly, happy super helpful staff.
Was a joy to stay here and can’t wait to be back.
Ticks all the boxes.  Good location and quiet considering its proximity to the highway.  Would definitely come back.
Loved our time here, wish we could have stayed longer. Clean amenities and great pools. The location is perfect with the creek and beach both an easy stroll. Our new favourite place.
Great spot on the river for a weekend getaway!

Totally enjoying my Birthday holiday weekend, great location amenities and staff but our waterfront cabin unfortunately has been crowded by our neighbour who has decided to park Right next to our balcony BBQ area so all privacy has gone having the van windows adjacent to our bbq cooking and verandah. Totally off the pad for van set-up!!
* Hi Scott thanks for that,we did mention to a passing staff member on Saturday afternoon about how close they were but he didn't offer any solutions,he just nodded and went on his way!
Very clean n tidy. Friendly staff. Kiosk has a good range. Bonus was the food truck there on the weekend. Close to the creek, beach and a great bush walking track.
We love the caravan park

Great place for a relaxing holiday to rejuvenate the soul.
Fishing, ball games on the soft sand, walking to the headland, or sitting back watching beautiful sunsets after a fantastic day.
Highly recommend.
Awesome park. Great ammenities!

This review is for the people running the park as a posed to the park it's self. I encountered Tim in uniform outside of the park at bunnings. He was polite and helped me get a heavy item into my car even know it wasn't his place of work. Top bloke!
Great park in a great location. Manager and employees are very friendly and go out of their way to make sure that everyone is having a good time. We'll definitely be back.

Big park with soooo much to do nearby or you can just chill at the pool or Talle Creek swimming, paddleboarding, fishing etc. We will definitely be back. A little bit of traffic noise but you forget about that pretty quickly when your feet hit the sand. Best showers I've experienced on our east coast trip.
Beautiful location. Crazy busy every time we visit. Park is well maintained
No need to go anywhere, nice walk and beach nearby
One of my fav spots x

Absolutely loved this park. There are 2 pools - 1 being an adults pool. Located right on the river/beach. Christened our double seater kayak and was perfect place to do so. There’s a small shop that sells necessities, coffees & booze which is really handy. Food / drinks trucks open on certain days . Wish it was dog friendly so we could bring our pup. Now we’ll have to find elsewhere 😔
Amazing. The best place. 👌

We rented a caravan from camplify and got a premium powered site, which was a site back from the waterfront ones. This has to be one of the best located caravan parks around. We had a great stay. We rented a paddle board across the creek ($30 = 90 mins, we hired a peddle buggy in the park ($15 =30 mins).

Great caravan/tourist park. Convenient location right on the creek, walking distance to the beach and surf club. 🌊🏄🏼‍♀️ Handy shop onsite with great coffee and snacks. ☕️ 🥐 Best part was the adults only heated pool with sunlounges, which was a nice quiet place to relax during the day. 👙🌴☀️This was the first time we had seen this at a caravan park, usually only at resorts. But a very welcome initiative for guests without kids! 😊
Great facilties, friendly staff , wonderful location  and reasonably priced. Would definitely stay here again
Beautiful, location perfect, truly “Simply the Best “ Shush don’t tell  anyone about it, or you’ll never get a site!
Great place to stay good walking trails about and beach for a walk or swim

Great location for a few days on the Gold Coast, a big park with clean amenities block and camp kitchen. There are three heated swimming pools to choose from and you can also swim in the river nearby.

Wat een grote camping. Veel aanbod om te doen. Helaas alles per half uur aftikken. Voor een camping van deze groote is het zeer teleurstellend dat ze geen wifi hebben. We kunnen goed zonder hoor. Maar in de tijd van nu is dit wel heel vreemd. Verder het sanitair was groot en veel. Maar heel veel stuk. Speelruimtevoor kinderen was ook beperkt. Op loopafstand wel veel te doen en bezichtigen.

Stayed here for one month. Very busy park with loads of kids.  All the rules in relation to no scooters, no electric bikes and helmets must be worn were clearly marked however despite the park having loads of staff moving around all through the day these rules were never enforced.  The bikes travelled around at a speed over the 8km that was marked for vehicles and there were constant kids, small toddlers on the roads riding bikes and scooters, playing chicken with the cars, with not a parent in sight.   The roads in van parks are for large vehicles manoeuvring larger vans, and the drivers should not have to also be worrying about someone’s kid screaming around a corner on a bike and ending up under their wheels. Would not stay there again because of it.
Despite having raised this with the park on the survey provided by them almost one month ago, we have received no feedback at all.
Not good enough
Rude office staff, tried to ring for 2 days but couldn't get through. packed in like sardines. Amenities clean but showers are terrible. travelling with family 3 vans 1 cabin.

We are a couple of friends going camping once a month, and try and plan our bookings well in advance. We have selected Tallebudgera and was looking forward to camping there.
Unfortunate circumstances prevented us from going a few times, and unfortunately the last time we had to give short notice due to the whole group coming down with covid. We we happily advised that our booking could be moved, but we will not be allowed to move our booking again even though I booked 6 months in advance and I had to make an immediate decision for the group. I did not expect that a holiday park would place this requirement on customers, as the deposit was paid, and we would happily pay the difference if there has been price increase, or a difference depending on the time of the year.  It is not normal for us to move our bookings often- but unfortunately this did happen.
There are no stipulation in any of the documentation that prevent us from moving our booking.
We were allowed to cancel, receive our deposit back, and rebook, but they were not able to move the booking. Logic tells me to refund and rebook is more work, costly and time consuming, than moving a booking - and I could not understand the purpose of this being imposed on us - and no explanation given - happily accepting my cancellation and comment that I would most likely  not return.
In principle I doubt we will opt to go to Tallebudgera Creek Tourist Park in the near future  -maybe ever, as Australia and even just the Gold Coast offer so many alternatives for people enjoying this lifestyle.
I felt disappointed that being busy and popular would resort to making people feel not valued and unwelcome. We camp a lot - and this experience was definitely a first.
Great park. Clean amenities. Walk around the creek or down to the surf club. On site shop for essentials. Surprisingly quiet even though there was very few empty sites.

Lost count of the number of times we have stayed at Tallebudgera Tourist Park. On this occasion, we stayed on a Premium powered site. These are a generous size for the bigger vans and still leave you with plenty of room. We usually try for a waterfront site. However, it was school holidays and these were booked. The park was still pretty quiet for holiday time. The staff are awesome in the office and there's staff running around throughout the day cleaning and doing the rubbish. The place is spotless. Then there's the location. It would have to be the pick of the caravan parks. You have an area for the kids with plenty of sand and no waves, then a short to the surf beach. Plus over the bridge, you have the Burleigh Heads National Park, which is a grratbwalk around the headland.
Great customer service,  had help with my bags and the staff really friendly.  Love staying here ❤️

We had the luxury of an ensuite site! It was very convenient to have our own bathroom with 3 young children! The staff provided fantastic school holiday activities that were great for all ages! Everyone was so helpful and the kids loved the humour of the staff! Such an enjoyable place to camp!
Great park.  Nice and quiet even though it was busy.  Friendly staff.  Great amenities.
Great van park along side river inlet with sandy beaches. Great clean amenities and friendly staff. Large sites some backing onto beach. A cafe with everything.

I telephoned to make my booking as it was after 5pm and I was concerned I might not arrive before the office closed. The staff were incredibly friendly both over the phone and in person when I rolled in just before 6pm.
The facilities were immaculate. Not a crumb in the camp kitchen and the bathrooms were easily accessible.
I am touring by bicycle and have a two-man tent. For A$54 for an unpowered site, I had expected it would be flat. Also, because I called over the phone, I didn't recognise that I’d be close to the highway and consequently, kept awake by traffic.
My dad loves this place and visits in his caravan every year, but I left feeling robbed.

Schöner Campingplatz mit guten Sanitäranlagen. Leider etwas teuer aber auf jeden Fall ein guter Aufenthalt. Fußläufig gibt es auch einen kleinen Nationalpark der auch einen Ausflug wert ist und natürlich viele schöne Strände. Die Mitarbeiterinnen waren auch sehr freundlich und hilfsbereit.

Many thanks to Tim from Gold Coast Tourist park for helping us jumpstart our car today. We were at Bunnings and our car battery died on us. Tim saw us and reached out for help and didn’t hesitate for a second. So grateful!!

Really nice set up, and facilities.
A little sad that the pool closes at sunset but what can you do
